subject: 1.0.0+N build-metadata-only format on master
notes: User requirement stated master between tags should produce 1.0.0+N. GitVersion's SemVer ordering constraint makes this unachievable — any commit past a tag must sort after that tag. ManualDeployment with empty label produces X.Y.(Z+1)-1+N. Resolved by explicitly documenting as ADR constraint, not misconfiguration.

subject: hotfix default label 'beta' vs {BranchName}
notes: Default GitFlowConfigurationBuilder sets hotfix label to 'beta' with IsReleaseBranch=true. User intent is {BranchName} label with IsReleaseBranch=false. The distinction between hotfix-as-release-branch and hotfix-as-label-carrier was not explicit in GitVersion documentation or default config.

subject: bugfix branch absent from default GitFlow config
notes: GitFlowConfigurationBuilder has no bugfix branch. User required bugfix/* with {BranchName} label. Added as new branch type mirroring feature/* structure. source-branches must reference exact ConfigurationConstants key names or ValidateConfiguration throws at build time.

subject: +semver: patch on Minor-configured branch
notes: Expected +semver: patch to downgrade develop's Minor increment to Patch. Actual behavior: branch increment wins when higher than message directive. +semver: patch is 'at least patch' not 'exactly patch'. Test corrected to assert 1.3.0-alpha.1 and renamed to document intent.

subject: build metadata count +N on feature branches includes parent commits
notes: Expected +0 immediately after branching feature from develop. Actual: +N where N = commits on parent branch since version source. The develop commit before feature branch is counted in feature's version source distance. Not documented explicitly in GitVersion docs.

subject: no-ff merge commit count on develop
notes: After MergeNoFF of feature branch (1 commit) into develop: expected alpha.2 (develop commit + merge commit). Actual: alpha.3 (develop commit + feature commit + merge commit). Feature commit is included in develop's history via no-ff merge parent traversal.

subject: source-branches: [] declaration on master
notes: The prims repo bug (1.62.0-alpha.261 instead of 1.61.0) was caused by missing source-branches: [] on master. Without it, lineage traversal follows merge parent through release to develop, picking up alpha label and commit count. source-branches: [] declares master as a primary branch with no inherited lineage. This is the root cause of the original issue that motivated this ADR.

subject: merge topology constraints for GitFlow
notes: User stated: no-FF merges only, never push directly to develop or master, never merge from develop/master into feature branches. These constraints are not enforceable via GitVersion.yml alone — they are branch protection rules and CI enforcement. GitVersion source-branches config partially models permitted lineage but does not block illegal merges.
